verses.text: God threatens frogs over all the land if Pharaoh refuses. Frogs warned. Judgment escalates against the stubborn.
God threatens frogs over all the land if Pharaoh refuses. Frogs warned. Judgment escalates against the stubborn.
God threatens frogs over all the land if Pharaoh refuses. Frogs warned. Judgment escalates against the stubborn.
God threatens frogs over all the land if Pharaoh refuses. Frogs warned. Judgment escalates against the stubborn.
Aaron stretches his hand, frogs cover the land; the magicians do the same. Plague comes. The land is overrun.
Aaron stretches his hand, frogs cover the land; the magicians do the same. Plague comes. The land is overrun.
Aaron stretches his hand, frogs cover the land; the magicians do the same. Plague comes. The land is overrun.
Pharaoh begs Moses to entreat God; Moses bids him set the time, 'that thou mayest know there is none like the LORD.' Tomorrow. God lifts the plague at the appointed word.
Pharaoh begs Moses to entreat God; Moses bids him set the time, 'that thou mayest know there is none like the LORD.' Tomorrow. God lifts the plague at the appointed word.

Pharaoh begs Moses to entreat God; Moses bids him set the time, 'that thou mayest know there is none like the LORD.' Tomorrow. God lifts the plague at the appointed word.
The frogs die in heaps; but seeing respite, Pharaoh hardens his heart. Respite abused. Mercy only emboldens the impenitent.

The third plague — lice from the dust; the magicians fail, owning, 'This is the finger of God.' God's finger. The counterfeiters meet their limit.

The fourth — swarms of flies, but Goshen is spared, 'that thou mayest know that I am the LORD.' Separation. God shields His own amid judgment.

The fourth — swarms of flies, but Goshen is spared, 'that thou mayest know that I am the LORD.' Separation. God shields His own amid judgment.
Pharaoh offers they sacrifice in the land; Moses refuses, lest they offend the Egyptians. No compromise. True worship will not be hemmed in.
Pharaoh offers they sacrifice in the land; Moses refuses, lest they offend the Egyptians. No compromise. True worship will not be hemmed in.
Pharaoh offers they sacrifice in the land; Moses refuses, lest they offend the Egyptians. No compromise. True worship will not be hemmed in.
Pharaoh lets them go a little way, asks prayer, then hardens again once the flies depart. Broken word. He bargains, then betrays.
